response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling passion — he built C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE, authored a D-language test runner and assertion library, and currently works on MCP code generators — but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM. Java appears only briefly and Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, and application-server experience are entirely absent, which are the central hard requirements for this enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE role.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Authored language tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts) showing genuine interest in developer tools
Proven work on complex, high-scale systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic) plus strong TDD/craftsmanship practices
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure; no evidence of deep JVM understanding
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise application-server ecosystem experience
Recent career centered on web/full-stack TypeScript and DLang, far from enterprise JVM tooling
